# Griha

> Policy-bounded AI execution system for home and edge environments. The product layer that inherits ContextOps governance, ContextBoundary egress control, and Sthala's narrate/compute constraint.

Griha demonstrates the family doctrine in a working system: probabilistic intelligence operates inside deterministic governance boundaries. LLMs interpret intent only — deterministic code owns all device execution.

License: Apache 2.0. Status: Proof of concept.

## Core

- [docs/architecture.md](https://github.com/kannanokannan/Griha/blob/main/docs/architecture.md): Architecture, failure narrative, governance inheritance
- [core/intent.py](https://github.com/kannanokannan/Griha/blob/main/core/intent.py): LLM intent extraction — narrate layer only
- [coordinator/main.py](https://github.com/kannanokannan/Griha/blob/main/coordinator/main.py): Deterministic validation and routing — compute layer
- [coordinator/egress.py](https://github.com/kannanokannan/Griha/blob/main/coordinator/egress.py): Egress control — ContextBoundary integration point

## Governance stack (inherited)

- [ContextOps](https://github.com/kannanokannan/ContextOps): AI context governance framework
- [ContextBoundary](https://github.com/kannanokannan/ContextBoundary): Egress governance specification
- [Sthala](https://github.com/kannanokannan/Sthala): On-prem AI reference framework
- [context-stack](https://github.com/kannanokannan/context-stack): Canonical terminology and decisions
